title: Joshua vs Dubois: two of the division's heaviest hands, and the question of who blinks first
summary: Between them, Anthony Joshua and Daniel Dubois have stopped 48 opponents. This is not a fight that promises to go the distance. It promises to find a chin.
body:
Put their numbers side by side and the picture is almost comic. Between them, Anthony Joshua and Daniel Dubois have won 52 fights and ended 48 of them early. This is not a contest built for the judges. It is a meeting of two of the heaviest hands at heavyweight, and the only real question is whose chin answers first.

Power on both sides

Dubois carries the more frightening rate. Of his 23 wins, 22 have come inside the distance, a knockout ratio of 96 per cent. Opponents are rarely still upright to hear a verdict. Joshua is not far behind. He has stopped 26 of his 29 victims, 90 per cent, and only three of his wins have ever reached the cards. Neither man is built to win dull decisions, and neither has had to learn how.

The other side of the ledger

Both also know what the canvas looks like. Joshua's record carries four defeats and Dubois's three, and the last five fights for each contain a knockout loss. These are not protected prospects. They are established heavyweights who hit hard, have been hurt, and keep coming forward. That shared vulnerability is what makes the fight live. There is no safe man here.

The physical edge

Joshua is the bigger figure on paper, 6ft 6in to Dubois's 6ft 5in, with an 82-inch reach against 78. Four inches of reach matters at this weight if he chooses to box behind the jab rather than trade. Dubois has youth on his side of the same orthodox stance, eight years of it, at 28 to Joshua's 36.

Where it turns

The data points to one clean fork. If Joshua uses his length and keeps Dubois at the end of his punches, the experience of 33 professional contests should tell. If the fight is fought up close, where a 96 per cent and a 90 per cent knockout rate collide, it becomes a test of who recovers. Recent results suggest either man can be the one who does not.
